commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use auto height for book cover images to prevent cropping on mobile

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(bot): recognize follow-ups after bot response as requests for help

"не допомагає", "не працює" etc. after a [BOT] response were being
classified as statements/noise and silently dropped. Added gate rule:
short feedback after [BOT] reply = follow-up requesting more help.

commit.message:
Parallelize crawl+kitphishr with ThreadPoolExecutor

Workers run crawl_url and run_kitphishr concurrently; DB writes happen
exclusively in the main thread via as_completed so SQLite never sees
concurrent writes. Worker count is configurable via crawl_workers in
settings (default 5).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix council hang on extended-thinking model timeouts

httpx read timeout doesn't enforce wall-clock deadlines, so models
with extended thinking (which stream internally) could block
indefinitely. Wrap requests with asyncio.wait_for() to enforce a
true wall-clock timeout. Also increases default timeout from 120s
to 3600s and adds per-model timeout configuration support in
council_models config (dict format with model + timeout keys).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 2: All 10 features — CSV, Cmd+K, Dashboard, Companies, Activity, Mobile, Scoring

feat: meal planning expansion — grocery shop, calendar-aware plans, batch cook, price scanner, recipe discovery, cooking reminders

Phase 1: Fix meal-rating schedule (add to SCHEDULE.md + set channel), calendar-aware planning endpoint (GET /calendar/meal-context with 7 override rules), batch cook intelligence (search_batch_friendly_recipes + generator logic).

Phase 2: Grocery shop skill (trolley add + resolve ambiguous + slot booking), meal plan → grocery integration (POST /meal-plan/shopping-list/to-trolley with dedup).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: check filePaths (not formData) for required file field validation

Server action was validating required file fields by looking for File
objects in formData, but those are now stripped client-side before the
action is called. Required file check now uses filePaths[field.key]
which holds the pre-uploaded Supabase Storage path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: explicit user dates must override default date ranges

When users specify a date (e.g., "in 2025", "last December"), the AI
was falling back to default ranges instead. Add directive to Critical
Requirements making user-specified dates always take priority.

Closes #26

commit.message:
feat: add BKMV revenue account classification override

Three-phase fix for King Kong Karmiel gas expense misclassification:

Phase 1 - Fix auto-classification bug (classification.ts):
- Add exact-match expense patterns for abbreviated Ravachit types ("גז", "חשמל", "מים")
- Make prefix fallback conservative: only apply when accountType is empty
  or equals accountName (parser fallback), not when it has a real but
  unrecognized value — prevents account key "214" (prefix 21) from being
  incorrectly mapped to revenue

Phase 2 - Fix override buttons (bkmvdata/page.tsx):
- Show error message instead of silently returning when matchedFranchisee
  is null in handleQuickClassify, handleReclassify, handleSaveRevenueAccounts
- Disable all quick-classify and reset buttons with Hebrew tooltip when
  no franchisee is matched

Phase 3 - Revenue recalculation on override:
- New POST /api/bkmvdata/recalculate-revenue endpoint that filters stored
  revenueAccounts by current DB classifications and recalculates
  revenueMonthlyBreakdown, ensuring reports reflect manual overrides
- Triggered automatically from classification change handlers when
  revenue category is involved

commit.message:
Claude/satellite web worker dv xx h (#18)

* Fix TLE loading stuck: reduce timeouts, add stale-cache fallback

Worst-case load time was ~14 min (8 layers × 8 retried fallbacks timing
out). Three changes:
- FETCH_TIMEOUT_DIRECT 8s→5s, FETCH_TIMEOUT_PROXY 12s→8s
- CORS proxy attempts 2→1 (no retry on last-resort fallbacks)
- Use stale localStorage cache when all network methods fail, instead of
  falling through to fully simulated orbits

Worst case is now ~4.5 min; users with any prior cached data load instantly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012c7untW3xPNdBtjJ6QPTr4

* Cap per-layer TLE fetch at 8s, remove dead allorigins proxy

allorigins times out hard at 12s per attempt. With the previous waterfall
(direct + JSON + 3 proxies × 2 retries), loading could take ~14 min when
celestrak.org fails in the browser. Adding a Promise.race cap of 8s per
layer limits worst case to 8 layers × 8s = ~64s regardless of how many
fallbacks are attempted. Also removes allorigins from CORS_PROXIES since
it is consistently unreachable.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012c7untW3xPNdBtjJ6QPTr4

* fix: invalidate bounding sphere after worker swaps position buffer

Three.js computes geometry.boundingSphere lazily from whatever positions
are in the buffer at first use. With zero-filled initial buffers, the
cached sphere has radius ~0 at origin. Setting boundingSphere = null
after each worker update forces Three.js to recompute it from real
satellite positions on the next raycast, restoring hover/click/tooltip.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012c7untW3xPNdBtjJ6QPTr4

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add v0.15.0 migration to fix tasks_au_active trigger asymmetry

The tasks_au_active trigger used WHEN new.deleted_at IS NULL, which
fires on both active→active updates AND deleted→active restores.
A restore attempt would call FTS5 'delete' on a row not in the index,
corrupting it (SQLITE_CORRUPT, error 267).

Fix narrows the WHEN clause to:
  old.deleted_at IS NULL AND new.deleted_at IS NULL
This ensures the trigger only fires on true active→active transitions,
symmetric with the tasks_au_softdelete trigger fix from v0.13.0.

v0.15.0 migration drops and recreates the trigger on existing databases.
migrate_fresh and upgrade_to_v0_13_0 also updated to use the correct clause.

Schema version bumped to 0.15.0; all related test assertions updated.

Fix sidecar reliability: retry apk add, kill stale Maestro drivers

Two issues found during E2E testing:

1. Alpine sidecars (unified-app, unified-maestro) silently failed to
   install openssh-client when DNS wasn't ready at container start.
   Added retry loop (5 attempts, 2s delay) and SSH verification gate
   that exits with error if install fails.

2. Stale Maestro Java processes held port 7001 on workers, blocking
   ADB port forwarding for subsequent test runs. Added cleanup step
   in run-test.sh that kills stale Maestro driver processes and
   clears ADB forwards before each test.

Rewrite SKILL.md v0.3.0: fully self-contained, no external scripts required

The .skill installer only extracts SKILL.md — external scripts were never
available at $SKILL_DIR/scripts/. This rewrite makes the skill work without
any external dependencies:

- Auto-detect .env in multiple fallback locations (skill dir, session
  paths, .local-plugins) so API keys are found regardless of install path
- Inline curl+python3 API calls for Ollama Cloud, OpenAI-compatible APIs,
  and local Ollama — no helper scripts needed
- Classification logic inlined as a rubric table Claude uses for reasoning
  (no task_classifier.py needed)
- Scripts in .skill/.zip remain as optional power-user utilities

Tested: Ollama Cloud (gemma3:4b) via inline curl, .env auto-detected from
.local-plugins path. Full round-trip working with only SKILL.md.

Extract the ad-hoc search spiral detection from implementer/debugger into
a reusable SearchSpiralGuard class in context_compressor.py. The guard
tracks file paths discovered by previous grep results and, when
search_grep is called more than 3 times within the last 6 tool calls,
blocks the search with a directive to call `think` and `read_file` on
specific previously-discovered files instead.

Applied consistently across all 6 agent loops: implementer, debugger,
archivist, security, release, and task_writer.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TBRJjLTMZxRbT2SYxWmDHR

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: glitchlab
repository.description: An agentic development engine for repo-agnostic, multi-agent code evolution under strict governance. Build Weird. Ship Clean.

commit.message:
Hybrid BRS+MCTS dead end: 2–46% across 6 configs, MCTS not viable (Iter 29)

Flat depth-1 MCTS with UCB1 and random opponents tested as root-level
vote alongside BRS. Sequential budget splits (70/30, 95/5), exact-tie
tiebreaker, and concurrent goroutines all failed. BRS depth hypersensitive
to budget; MCTS random sampling produces wrong preferences against optimal
play. Infrastructure retained: BRSResult, bestMoveBRS, mctsSearch, mcts.go.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 点線間隔を狭く・「分」フォント縮小・ズームアウト時の森林表示拡大

- line-dasharray [2,3]→[2,1.5] で点線の空白を狭く
- 「7分」→数字5xl+「分」2xlに分離しフォントサイズ調整
- MAX_RADIUS 15km→30km、MAX_MARKERS 30→50でズームアウト時に広範囲の森林を表示

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: HELO goto wait_until을 domcontentloaded로 변경

nexacro 앱이 지속적 네트워크 요청을 하므로 networkidle 조건이
영원히 충족되지 않음. domcontentloaded로 변경하고 nexacro 로딩은
wait_for_nexacro()에서 별도 처리.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: dok-workflow

commit.message:
Session 25: waypoint UI consistency, login date fix, gallery share link

- Waypoint UI: all 0-night stops now render as grey dashed card regardless
  of whether they reached 0 via _toggleWaypoint or the minus button.
  phaseHeaderHtml detects effective totalNights===0 early; removePhaseDay
  auto-sets waypointOverrides when hitting 0 nights.

- Login end date: pre-login init now applies phaseExtraDays total adjustment
  to the displayed end date so login screen reflects the actual schedule
  (not the raw last TRIP_DAYS date).

- Gallery share link (?gallery=1): friends can view photos without login.
  Bypasses auth, loads Supabase state, boots viewer mode, hides all tabs
  except Gallery. Share button in gallery header copies the link.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove hardcoded 1M cycle limit from tracer

Match Jolt's behavior: let the tracer run freely until program
termination (ECALL or PC stall). Trace length is validated at
proving time against max_trace_length instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
